user_query:
I’m launching a blog as an ex–big-tech backend engineer who left to run a one-person SaaS serving indie developers (currently ~$12k MRR). Please read the inputs in input/ and produce three deliverables under output/ that I can ship immediately.

Inputs to read:
- input/persona.md (my background, audience, constraints)
- input/trends.json (a snapshot of current topics my readers care about; treat these as potential demand signals)
- input/draft_notes.md (scrappy ideas and fragments)

Deliverables to create:
1) output/strategy.json — a 90-day content strategy that includes exactly these fields:
   - niche: string
   - perspective: string (at least 120 characters) that clearly reflects: "one-person SaaS", "indie developers", and "$12k MRR"
   - pillars: array of 3–5 unique strings (these will be my enduring content pillars)
   - cadence: object with { posts_per_week: 3, newsletter: "weekly" }
   - newsletter_plan: string (how the newsletter deepens the relationship and drives return readership)
   - monetization_roadmap: array of at least 2 strings (coherent with the blog’s value; e.g., newsletter, course, consulting, productized services)
   - top_trends_snapshot: array of at least 3 strings selected from input/trends.json that you believe align with my audience

2) output/editorial_calendar.csv — a 4-week editorial calendar with header row:
   date,pillar,working_title,post_type,primary_reader_question,search_intent
   - Use Monday 2026-05-04 as the start of Week 1
   - Exactly 12 rows (3 posts per week for 4 weeks)
   - post_type coverage must include at least one of each (case-insensitive): Argument, Tutorial, Story, Research Synthesis
   - Each row’s pillar must be one of the pillars from output/strategy.json
   - At least one working_title must incorporate one item from strategy.json.top_trends_snapshot

3) output/blog_post.md — a flagship long-form post, tied to one of the trends you selected and shaped by my perspective. Must follow all of these:
   - Begin with YAML front matter delimited by --- lines including keys: title (string), description (string), keywords (array of 3–8 short strings), slug (kebab-case string)
   - After front matter, the first non-empty line must start with "Hook:" and be ≤200 characters
   - Body is ≥800 words
   - Include at least four H2 sections (lines starting with "## ")
   - Include a section titled exactly "## Reader Q&A" containing 5 pairs in list form with lines that start with "- Q:" and "- A:" (5 questions and their answers)
   - Include a final section titled exactly "## What to do next" that mentions both "newsletter" and "course"
   - The first keyword in front matter is the "primary keyword" and must appear in the body between 2 and 6 times (case-insensitive)
   - The slug must be strictly kebab-case (lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only) and include at least one hyphen
   - At least one of the front matter keywords must be exactly equal (case-insensitive) to one item from strategy.json.top_trends_snapshot
   - The body must contain at least one of these perspective markers: "from my experience", "I learned", or "in my own work"
   - The body must contain at least one of the pillar names from strategy.json.pillars

Notes:
- Do not over-optimize for keywords; write for humans first.
- Keep everything in English.
- Only write files to output/; do not write anywhere else.
